kba_problem_summary: Intermittent loss of communication on the I/O railbus when using 12P1795 dual extender cables in horizontal simplex 8-wide carrier applications, primarily triggered by cable bending or twisting during installation.
severity_justification: Intermittent I/O railbus communication loss degrades control performance for downstream I/O cards without causing immediate plant shutdown or safety impact.
occurrence_justification: Issue manifests mainly during staging/installation under specific mechanical stress (bending/twisting); not a spontaneous operational failure.
detectability_justification: Intermittent and installation-dependent nature makes proactive detection difficult without targeted railbus monitoring or post-installation testing.

modifiers_applied.reason: Procedural precautions and optional redundancy cable reduce likelihood and impact

architecture_level_description: Livello 1: Controllo base (controller, I/O card, railbus)
workaround_description: Avoid sharp bends and twisting near connectors during installation; add a second cable for redundancy.
notes: KBA dated 2005; issue is mechanical/installation-related rather than a firmware or systemic software defect. No safety (SIS) involvement and no patch provided.
